zh.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour是Mauger le Vavasour III的兒子，於大約1131年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾哈茲列武德 白 塔德卡斯特。他於1154年與Matilda Perry在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾約克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1154年被列為judge during reign of henry ii。他去世於1191年6月29日之前在英國英格蘭越可爾斯吉普頓，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 迪拉利迪拉利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour是Mauger le Vavasour III的儿子，于大约1131年出生在英国英格兰越可爾哈茲列武德 白 塔德卡斯特。他于1154年与Matilda Perry在英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾約克结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1154年被列为judge during reign of henry ii。他去世于1191年6月29日之前在英国英格兰越可爾斯吉普頓，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 迪拉利迪拉利。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Vavasour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: status name for a feudal tenant ranking immediately below a baron, Old French vavas(s)our, vavasseur, Latin vassus vassorum ‘vassal of vassals’.

variants.name: Bavister
meanings: English: Titford suggests that this is a late variant of Vavasour . It would be a dissimilated form of Vavister and Vavester, variants of Vavasour that are evidenced in Lincs, also in Cambs, whence the name spread into Beds. Compare Willa Vavister, 1579 in IGI (Scopwick, Lincs); Thomas Vavister, 1615 in IGI (Cranwell, Lincs); Mary Vavester, 1708, Joseph Vavisor, 1738 in IGI (New Sleaford, Lincs).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour was born about 1131, in Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Mauger le Vavasour III. He married Matilda Perry in 1154, in York, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. In 1154, his occupation is listed as judge during reign of henry ii. He died before 29 June 1191, in Skipton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Tipperary, County Tipperary, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour est né environ 1131, à Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Mauger le Vavasour III. Il a épousé Matilda Perry en 1154, à York, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. En 1154, sa profession de son indiquée est judge during reign of henry ii. Il est décédé avant 29 juin 1191, à Skipton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Tipperary, County Tipperary, Irlande.

it.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour è nato il circa 1131 nel Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Mauger le Vavasour III. Lui sposato Matilda Perry nel 1154, nel York, North Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1154, la sua occupazione è indicata come judge during reign of henry ii. Morì prima del 29 giugno 1191, nel Skipton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Tipperary, County Tipperary, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasourは約1131年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアハズルオオッド ビー タドカスターでMauger le Vavasour IIIの息子として生まれた。1154年にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアヨークでMatilda Perryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1154年，職業はjudge during reign of henry iiと記録されている。彼は1191年6月29日以前にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアスキプトンで死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ティップエラリーティップエラリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Hazlewood By Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 하즐루드 비 타드카스터
ko.Hazlewood, Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 하즐루드 비 타드카스터
ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour(은)는 대략 1131년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 하즐루드 비 타드카스터에서 Mauger le Vavasour III의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1154년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 요르크에서 Matilda Perry(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1154년, 그의 직업은 judge during reign of henry ii이었다. 그는 1191년 6월 29일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 스키프턴에서 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 티페라리 티페라리에 묻혔다.

pt.York, North Yorkshire, England, United K: York, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour nasceu aproximadamente 1131, em Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Mauger le Vavasour III. Ele casou-se com Matilda Perry em 1154, em York, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1154, sua profissão aparece como judge during reign of henry ii. Ele faleceu antes 29 de junho de 1191, em Skipton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Tipperary, County Tipperary, Irlanda.

ru.Hazlewood By Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Engla: хазльвуд би тадкастир, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Hazlewood, Yorkshire, England: хазльвуд би тадкастир, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour родился около 1131 г. в местности хазльвуд би тадкастир, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Mauger le Vavasour III. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda Perry в 1154 г. в местности ерк, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. В 1154 году он работал по профессии judge during reign of henry ii. Он умер перед 29 июнь 1191 г. в местности скиптон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности типпирэри, коунти типпирэри, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Sir William Le Vavasour nació aproximadamente 1131, en Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Mauger le Vavasour III. Se casó con Matilda Perry en 1154, en York, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1154, su ocupación registrada era judge during reign of henry ii. Murió antes de 29 de junio de 1191, en Skipton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Tipperary, County Tipperary, Irlanda.
